question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a crafted message payload containing binary data that, when stored via toBytes(transactionBody.getMessage()), causes downstream consumers reading from topic_message to interpret the content as a different encoding or inject control characters, leading to data integrity violations in mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a maximally large message payload to toBytes(transactionBody.getMessage()) such that the resulting byte array causes excessive memory allocation in the importer pipeline, leading to out-of-memory conditions or denial of service for other topic message processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with an empty message body such that toBytes(transactionBody.getMessage()) returns a zero-length byte array, causing the topic_message table to store a null or empty message that violates the NOT NULL constraint on the message column, leading to a parser crash or silent data corruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the receipt's topicRunningHash is set to an all-zero byte array, causing toBytes(receipt.getTopicRunningHash()) to store a zeroed running hash in the topic_message table, allowing the attacker to break the cryptographic chain of running hashes and make the mirror node report a falsified hash state to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt containing a topicSequenceNumber of 0 or a negative-equivalent value, causing topicMessage.setSequenceNumber(receipt.getTopicSequenceNumber()) to store an invalid sequence number, breaking the monotonic ordering invariant of topic messages and corrupting sequence-based lookups in topic_message_lookup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt topicSequenceNumber that duplicates an already-persisted sequence number for the same topic, causing entityListener.onTopicMessage(topicMessage) to attempt inserting a duplicate row, leading to a database constraint violation that halts the importer or silently drops the duplicate message?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt topicRunningHashVersion of 0, triggering the special-case logic receipt.getTopicRunningHashVersion() == 0 ? 1 : (int) receipt.getTopicRunningHashVersion() to store version 1 instead of the actual version, causing downstream consumers that rely on running hash version to use the wrong hash algorithm when verifying message integrity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt topicRunningHashVersion equal to DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION (3), causing the condition receipt.getTopicRunningHashVersion() != DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION to be false and topicMessage.setRunningHashVersion() to never be called, resulting in a null runningHashVersion stored in the database that breaks consumers expecting a non-null version?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt topicRunningHashVersion set to an arbitrarily large integer value, causing (int) receipt.getTopicRunningHashVersion() to overflow when cast, storing a negative or incorrect running hash version in the topic_message table and misleading consumers about the hash algorithm used?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a chunkInfo where chunkNum is set to a value greater than chunkTotal, causing topicMessage.setChunkNum() and topicMessage.setChunkTotal() to store logically inconsistent chunk metadata, leading to incorrect reassembly of fragmented messages by downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with chunkInfo.getNumber() set to 0 or a negative value, causing topicMessage.setChunkNum() to store an invalid chunk number that breaks chunk reassembly logic in consumers, leading to permanent loss of the fragmented message?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with chunkInfo.getTotal() set to Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing topicMessage.setChunkTotal() to store an extremely large total chunk count, causing consumers to wait indefinitely for chunk reassembly and exhausting memory or connection resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a chunkInfo that has hasInitialTransactionID() returning true but with a crafted initialTransactionID whose toByteArray() produces a malformed protobuf byte sequence, causing topicMessage.setInitialTransactionId() to store corrupt bytes that break downstream deserialization of the initial_transaction_id column?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a chunkInfo containing an initialTransactionID with an extremely large byte representation, causing topicMessage.setInitialTransactionId(chunkInfo.getInitialTransactionID().toByteArray()) to store an oversized blob in the initial_transaction_id column, leading to database storage exhaustion or insert failures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple transactions with the same initialTransactionID in their chunkInfo but different chunkNum values, causing the mirror node to store multiple partial chunks that appear to belong to the same logical message, allowing the attacker to inject spurious chunks that corrupt the reassembled message content seen by consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a chunkInfo where chunkNum equals chunkTotal equals 1 but the message content differs from the original single-chunk message, causing the mirror node to store a tampered single-chunk message that consumers treat as a complete, unmodified message?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a topicID that references a non-existent or deleted topic, causing getEntity() to return an EntityId for a non-existent topic and entityListener.onTopicMessage() to persist a topic message associated with a ghost topic ID, polluting the topic_message table with orphaned records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a topicID containing shard/realm/num values that encode to a very large EntityId, causing getEntity() to return an EntityId that collides with or shadows a legitimate topic's ID in the entity tracking system, leading to misattribution of messages to the wrong topic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addCommonEntityIds()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the payerAccountId is set to a spoofed account ID, causing addCommonEntityIds() to call recordItem.addEntityId(transaction.getPayerAccountId()) with a forged payer, leading to incorrect entity transaction tracking that misattributes the transaction to a different account in the entity_transaction table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addCommonEntityIds()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the nodeAccountId is set to a non-existent node account, causing addCommonEntityIds() to call recordItem.addEntityId(transaction.getNodeAccountId()) with an invalid node ID, leading to phantom entity transaction records that corrupt the entity_transaction audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where recordItem.isSuccessful() returns true despite the underlying transaction being semantically invalid, bypassing the !recordItem.isSuccessful() guard at line 53 and causing doUpdateTransaction() to persist a topic message for a transaction that should have been rejected, leading to phantom messages in the topic_message table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record stream item where isBlockstream() returns false but the receipt's topicRunningHash is empty, bypassing the blockstream-specific guard at line 58 and causing doUpdateTransaction() to call toBytes(receipt.getTopicRunningHash()) on an empty ByteString, storing a zero-length running hash that breaks the hash chain integrity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the receipt's topicRunningHash is set to a byte array of incorrect length (not 48 bytes as expected for SHA-384), causing toBytes(receipt.getTopicRunningHash()) to store a malformed running hash that passes no length validation, allowing the attacker to inject arbitrary-length byte sequences as the running hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a receipt topicRunningHash that is a valid-length but cryptographically incorrect hash (not matching the actual hash of the previous running hash + message), causing the mirror node to store and serve a falsified running hash to consumers who rely on it for message integrity verification?
